title: 2023 amendments to the Saudi Personal Data Protection Law
title_ar: تعديلات نظام حماية البيانات الشخصية لعام 2023
official_title: Royal Decree No. M/148 of 5/9/1444H amending the Personal Data Protection Law
official_title_ar: المرسوم الملكي رقم م/148 وتاريخ 5/9/1444هـ بتعديل نظام حماية البيانات الشخصية
citation: Royal Decree No. M/148 of 5/9/1444H (27 March 2023) amending the Personal Data Protection Law
summary: A substantive amendment package adopted before the PDPL entered into force. It introduced legitimate interest as a lawful basis for non sensitive data, replaced the restrictive transfer regime with a conditions based one, and removed the controller registration fee mechanism, aligning the law more closely with international practice.
impact_note: The amendments materially lowered friction for businesses ahead of enforcement. Legitimate interest became available as a lawful basis for processing non sensitive personal data, and cross border transfers moved from a default prohibition to a framework of conditions and safeguards elaborated by the transfer regulations. Entities that designed compliance programmes against the original 2021 text should reassess their lawful basis mapping and transfer mechanics against the amended text, which is the version now in force.

entity_types: foreign entities processing data of Saudi residents

notes: Replace the source link with the gazette publication of 4 April 2023 and confirm the issue number.
